race.name: Valdres Skimaraton
race.tagline: Sixty-seven kilometers from Aurdal to Beitostølen through the Valdres valley — a classic marathon beneath the Jotunheimen giants where Norway's mountain heart beats loudest.

vitals.aid_stations: Multiple feed stations with warm drinks and energy food

climate.description: March race date in the Valdres valley means cold but lengthening daylight. The Jotunheimen mountains shelter the valley but temperatures can still drop well below zero. Typically -12°C to -3°C. Snow is reliable at this altitude and time of year.
challenges: Cold mountain winds descending from Jotunheimen
Variable snow conditions as March sun warms exposed sections
Altitude gain in later kilometers adds fatigue
Long point-to-point format demands pacing discipline

course.primary: Point-to-point from the valley town of Aurdal climbing toward the mountain resort of Beitostølen. The course follows traditional ski trails through the Valdres valley with a gradual net climb, passing forests and open mountain terrain beneath the Jotunheimen massif. The final kilometers offer panoramic mountain views.
course.surface: Machine-groomed classic tracks through valley and mountain terrain
course.grooming: Good Norwegian standard, Langløpscupen Innlandet quality
features: Jotunheimen mountain panorama in final third
Gradual net climb toward Beitostølen at ~900m
Classic Norwegian mountain and forest terrain
Finish at Beitostølen — a major Nordic skiing center
Part of Langløpscupen Innlandet regional series

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Maximum scenery anchored by Jotunheimen views. Strong distance and elevation scores. Limited by modest field size and primarily regional draw. Beitostølen finish adds logistical convenience.

history.summary: A classic-technique marathon through the Valdres valley in central Norway, running from Aurdal to the mountain resort of Beitostølen. Part of the Langløpscupen Innlandet regional long-distance series. The race showcases some of Norway's most dramatic mountain scenery beneath the Jotunheimen range, Norway's highest mountain massif.
notable_facts: Finishes at Beitostølen, one of Norway's premier Nordic skiing centers
The Jotunheimen massif includes Galdhøpiggen, Scandinavia's highest peak
Part of Langløpscupen Innlandet, the regional long-distance cup
Valdres is one of Norway's traditional mountain districts
Net uphill course from valley floor toward mountain plateau
